title: Coffee Table with Train Table/Storage
summary: In this plan you’ll see how to build a coffee table with a simple, modern base and train table on top, which can also be used as storage.
description: In this plan you’ll see how to build a coffee table with a simple, modern base and train table on top, which can also be used as storage. This can also be used without the storage above, but it makes a great place to store things like board games and other things not used daily.

materials.name: Wood stain in colour of choice
Variety of acrylic paints (I used light green, dark green, blue, charcoal, and white)

steps.title: Prepare base
steps.body: Once pieces are cut to size according to cut list, drill pocket holes into all of the 1-1/2"x 1-1/2" pieces for the base, except the 12" ones. Sand all pieces.

steps.title: Prepare tabletop surround
steps.body:
Drill pocket holes into all four pieces, plus an additional two holes on the sides of the longer pieces to create a "box." Attach all four pieces using 1-1/4" pocket screws.
Sand all pieces smooth with 120 grit sandpaper.

steps.title: Build frame base - part 1
steps.body: Using 45" and 21" pieces of the 1-1/2"x1-1/2" boards, create two frames as illustrated in image using 2-1/2" pocket screws.

steps.title: Build frame base - part 2
steps.body: Attach 1-1/2"x1-1/2"x21" pieces to the frames using 2-1/2" pocket screws.

steps.title: Build bottom supports
steps.body: Attach the 3/4"x1-1/2"x21" boards to the base frame to create a support for the bottom of the tabletop.

steps.title: Add plywood bottom
steps.body: You can use screws for this, but it should be a tight enough fit that you don't need to.

steps.title: Attach train table surround
steps.body: Stain the entire thing in the colour of your choice, then, using 1-1/2" pocket screws, attach top piece.

steps.title: Prepare tabletop
steps.body: Add stoppers to the tabletop so that the top doesn't slide around. Stain in colour of choice.

steps.title: Paint base
steps.body: Using a matte black paint of your choice, paint the base.

steps.title: Draw train table landscape
steps.body: Using a pencil, draw a road, lake, trees, and whatever else you'd like as part of your train table landscape on the plywood base.

steps.title: Paint lansdcape
steps.body: Using acrylic paint and small craft paintbrushes, paint the grass, water, road, etc.

steps.title: Add trains!
steps.body: Add in your trains and let the kiddos play! When they're done, just add the top back on!
